editable-table.notes: Use dxDataGrid for flat editable records. Use dxTreeList when rows have parent-child hierarchy.

hierarchical-table.notes: Use dxTreeList for expandable parent-child rows. Use grouped dxDataGrid only when hierarchy is shallow and not structural.

form.notes: Use dxForm for labeled editor layout, validation groups, and responsive form columns.

command-bar.notes: Use dxToolbar for page actions, filters, search controls, and grouped commands.

modal-workflow.notes: Use dxPopup for blocking workflow, edit dialogs, confirmations with content, and review panels.

drawer-detail-panel.notes: Use dxDrawer for persistent navigation or side detail panels. Use dxPopup for temporary modal detail.

tabbed-content.notes: Use dxTabPanel when content panes are owned by the widget. Use dxTabs when tabs only drive external content.

step-workflow.notes: Use dxStepper to expose workflow progress and dxForm for each step's inputs.

select-input.notes: Use dxSelectBox for single choice from a moderate list.

tag-input.notes: Use dxTagBox for multi-select tags with search and removable selections.

date-input.notes: Use dxDateBox for one date or date-time value. Use dxDateRangeBox for start/end range workflows.

notifications.notes: Use notify for simple status feedback. Use dxToast when a configured reusable widget is needed.

load-state.notes: Use dxLoadPanel for blocking page or panel work. Use dxLoadIndicator inline for small loading regions.

menus.notes: Use dxMenu for visible command navigation. Use dxContextMenu for right-click or overflow actions.

navigation.notes: Choose based on hierarchy, density, and responsive shell behavior.
